title: Hilleria secunda
intro: Hilleria secunda, also known as the Hilleria plant or Candelilla, is a succulent plant species native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the reduction of inflammation and the improvement of cardiovascular health, due to its antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Hilleria secunda include saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Hilleria secunda include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Hilleria secunda is classified within the family Lamiaceae. This family, commonly known as the mint or deadnettle family, includes a wide variety of plants, many of which are aromatic.
